loop_cards.who_has: the small, fast memory on or near the CPU that holds frequently used data to cut slow RAM fetches
the technique of overlapping the fetch, decode and execute of consecutive instructions to raise throughput
an independent processing unit able to run its own fetch-decode-execute cycle
the many-core processor that performs the same operation on large data sets in parallel
carrying out multiple computations simultaneously by splitting a task across cores
the architecture with one shared memory and bus for both data and instructions
the architecture with separate memories and buses for data and instructions, allowing simultaneous access
the processor type with many complex, variable-length instructions and complexity in hardware (e.g. x86)
the processor type with few simple fixed-length instructions aiming for one per cycle, used in ARM mobile devices
the CPU component that performs arithmetic, logic and shift operations
the CPU component that decodes instructions, generates control signals and synchronises with the clock
the register holding the address of the next instruction, incremented each cycle
the register that stores the immediate result of an ALU calculation
the use of secondary storage as extra RAM when physical RAM is full
the property of memory that loses its contents when power is removed (e.g. RAM)
the small, very fast store inside the CPU that holds data during processing

callers.clue: Performs arithmetic, logic and shift operations.
Decodes instructions and generates control signals.
Holds the address of the next instruction.
Holds the address of the location to read from or write to.
Holds the data or instruction just fetched from memory.
Holds the current instruction while it is decoded and executed.
Stores the immediate result of an ALU calculation.
Small fast memory on/near the CPU holding frequently used data.
Overlapping fetch, decode and execute to raise throughput.
An independent unit that runs its own FDE cycle.
Many-core processor applying one operation across large data sets.
Pulses per second that synchronise the CPU, measured in Hz.
One shared memory and bus for data and instructions.
Separate memories and buses for data and instructions.
Many complex, variable-length instructions; complexity in hardware.
Few simple fixed-length instructions; used in ARM devices.
Stores data as magnetised patterns on a spinning platter.
Non-volatile EEPROM storage with no moving parts.
A laser reads pits and lands via reflected light.
Uses secondary storage as extra RAM when RAM is full.

quickfire.clue: The bus that is unidirectional, carrying addresses from CPU to memory.
The register pair you must never swap: address vs data.
The step in fetch most often forgotten, along with copying MDR to CIR.
Non-volatile memory holding the bootstrap/BIOS firmware.
Volatile memory holding currently running programs and data.
The cache level that is fastest and smallest, on the core.
Excessive paging between RAM and disk that grinds a system to a halt.
The part of a task that must run sequentially, limiting the speed-up from adding cores.
Storage access type of tape: you must read through in sequence.
Technique flash memory uses to spread writes and prolong cell life.
Output device that builds objects layer by layer.
The general rule for 'choose a device' questions: link each characteristic to the requirement.
